title: SDL PlatformConnect
description: A PlatformConnect is a materialized inter-service connection at layer_platform — the DL inter-Container Connect owned by the platform Container. PlatformConnect objects are generated by the synthesis tool by reading all service dependencies.sdl.json files; they are not hand-authored. Each PlatformConnect represents an edge in the cross-service dependency graph and includes a resolution status so engineers can spot mismatches (a service calls another that has no matching SDL entry).

dl.notes: PlatformConnect = inter-Container Connect owned by the platform Container. Per DL scoping rule: the Connect is owned by the Container that contains both source and target. Source and target here are service Containers (ServiceActors) inside the platform Container. Generated by synthesis, not hand-authored.

id.description: Unique identifier for this platform-level connection. Convention: 'source->target' or 'source->target:dependency_id'.

source_service_id.description: The service that initiates this call. Must reference a service id in the platform manifest.

target_service_id.description: The service being called. Must reference a service id in the platform manifest.

style.description: Whether this connection is synchronous (caller waits) or asynchronous (event-driven, fire-and-forget).

dependency_ref.description: Reference back to the source Dependency declaration in the service's dependencies.sdl.json.

service_id.description: The service that declared this dependency.

dependency_id.description: The dependency id in that service's dependencies.sdl.json.

status.description: Resolution status of this connection. 'resolved' = both source and target have SDL descriptions and interfaces match. 'unresolved' = dependency declared but no SDL found for the target. 'mismatch' = SDL found but interface or protocol does not align.
